message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_kthvalue autograd via index-routing backward [bd-0juq]

tensor_kthvalue used to extract values, sort indices, build the
value as a requires_grad=false leaf, and rebuild a non-grad leaf
for the index — silently severing autograd through the value
branch. PyTorch's torch.kthvalue IS differentiable: gradient
routes back to the single input position selected by sort.

Wrap the value branch in tensor_apply_function with single-index
routing backward:
   grad_input[idx] = grad_value
   grad_input[other] = 0
where idx = sorted indices[k - 1]. The index branch stays a
non-grad leaf (integer-valued).

Adds kthvalue_propagates_gradient_to_routed_index regression test:
  x = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5], k = 3 → 3rd smallest is 3 at idx 0
  Under sum loss, grad_input = [1, 0, 0, 0, 0].

Closes frankentorch-0juq. ft-api lib tests: 1163 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-0ji","title":"Bound conformance parser diagnostics for fuzzed string inputs","description":"testing-fuzzing audit found unbounded diagnostic echo in crates/ft-conformance/src/lib.rs parser helpers. parse_binary_op, parse_dtype, parse_device, and parse_keyset include the full rejected input in error strings. These functions consume values from JSON conformance fixtures and fuzzed/adversarial inputs; a single oversized token can produce multi-megabyte error/log output even though the harness already has bounded_diagnostic for legacy oracle errors. Fix by bounding rejected parser tokens through the existing bounded_diagnostic helper, add regression tests with oversized strings, and preserve existing valid parse behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T19:53:08.763080564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:58:25.748376581Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:58:25.746265910Z","close_reason":"Bounded conformance parser diagnostics for oversized fuzzed tokens","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-0juq","title":"tensor_kthvalue severs autograd; index-routing backward needed","description":"tensor_kthvalue at lib.rs:12512 returns the k-th smallest element of a 1-D tensor. The value branch is just input[idx] where idx is the rank-k sorted index. PyTorch's torch.kthvalue IS differentiable: grad_input[idx] = grad_value, all other positions zero.

Wrap value branch in tensor_apply_function with this single-index routing backward. Index branch stays a non-grad leaf (integer).","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_kthvalue's value branch in tensor_apply_function with single-index routing backward: grad_input[idx] = grad_value where idx is the sort-rank-k index. The index branch stays a non-grad leaf. Added kthvalue_propagates_gradient_to_routed_index regression test (x=[3,1,4,1,5], k=3 → 3rd smallest is at idx 0 with value 3, grad_input = [1,0,0,0,0]). ft-api lib tests: 1163.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T22:02:20.918992929Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T22:04:14.853931515Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T22:04:14.851647564Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-0pv","title":"Add gradient clipping utilities: clip_grad_norm_, clip_grad_value_","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:14:13.201408803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:24:04.887255010Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:24:04.886889858Z","close_reason":"Implemented clip_grad_norm_ and clip_grad_value_ with 6 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-0tw","title":"Add sparse tensor storage and basic sparse ops","description":"## Progress

### Completed
1. SparseCOOTensor in ft-core with indices [sparse_dim, nnz], values [nnz, *dense_dims], coalesced flag
2. SparseCSRTensor in ft-core with crow_indices, col_indices, values
3. SparseTensorError enum with comprehensive validation errors
4. AutogradError::SparseTensor variant for error propagation
5. ft-api sparse tensor methods:
   - sparse_coo_tensor(coords, values, shape, dtype)
   - sparse_coo_tensor_from_indices(indices, values, shape, sparse_dim, coalesced)
   - sparse_csr_tensor(crow_indices, col_indices, values, shape)
   - tensor_to_sparse_coo(input) - dense to sparse conversion
   - sparse_coo_to_dense / sparse_csr_to_dense
6. ft-kernel-cpu sparse operations:
   - sparse_coo_matmul_dense_f64 - sparse @ dense matmul
   - sparse_coo_coalesce - merge duplicate indices
   - sparse_coo_add - element-wise addition
7. GradientValue enum (Dense/Sparse) in ft-autograd for unified gradient representation
8. sparse_gradients field in TensorBackwardReport for sparse gradient storage
9. Embedding.sparse field in ft-nn with with_options(sparse) constructor
10. SparseAdam now checks report for sparse gradients and iterates efficiently over COO entries

### Tests (11 core sparse + 177 optim + 299 core/kernel passing)
- 8 in ft-core: COO/CSR creation, to_dense roundtrip, error handling
- 3 in ft-kernel-cpu: matmul, coalesce, add
- All existing tests pass with new sparse gradient infrastructure

### Remaining for full sparse gradient flow
- Wire sparse backward path in IndexSelect when source tensor is marked sparse
- Test sparse gradient round-trip with Embedding + SparseAdam
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:24.369221708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T02:08:40.464076761Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T02:08:40.462288997Z","close_reason":"Wired sparse backward path in IndexSelect (sparse_dim=1, dim=0) — Embedding(sparse=true) now surfaces SparseCOOTensor on TensorBackwardReport.sparse_gradient(weight); SparseAdam updates only touched rows; full Embedding+SparseAdam roundtrip test added in ft-optim; 4 sparse-grad tests added across ft-nn/ft-optim, all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-18a","title":"Align proposed architecture crate map with current workspace layers","description":"Update PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md so its crate map and runtime flow mention the in-tree ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data layers instead of stopping at ft-api/ft-conformance.","notes":"Updated the proposed architecture doc so the crate map includes ft-nn, ft-optim, and ft-data, and the runtime flow now acknowledges those higher-level layers before conformance/durability.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:09:02.557560325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-28T05:09:21.467027815Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T05:09:21.466400527Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
